title: Rewrite command-auto-yee as a relational TargetSource with peers
description: Today's command-auto-yee synthesizes per-pair <yee-mix>es — a hack. Rewrite as a single TargetSource that emits Mixables with peers={commander, commandable} per wiring. Each pair is one Mixable target; the applier protocol's new context.peers slot (yee-v2-peers) gives the applier access to both ends. Tests the mixable contract's peers field end-to-end. Pairs with mixbl-command-auto and mixbl-peers-semantics and yee-v2-peers.

title: Decision: command-auto as meta-applier vs TargetSource
description: Two architectures compete: (A) command-auto-yee stays a meta-applier that synthesizes child <yee-listen> instances per pair; (B) command-auto-yee becomes a TargetSource emitting peer-Mixables. This decision picks one. Recommendation: B — it makes command-auto a first-class target source rather than a special case of yee-mix composition, and exercises the relational semantics the family needs. Pairs with comay-relational-source.

title: Adopt yee-there / yee-goeth for wiring arrival and unwiring
description: Apply the lifecycle contracts. A wiring is 'there' when the commander's listener is bound AND the commandable's method is resolved. A wiring 'goeth away' on unapply — wait for in-flight invocations to drain before tearing down the listener. Pairs with yee-v2-lifecycle.
